findings.problem: Once Tomas expels Mina in the lab, the second scene largely re-argues the same route/use thesis while the freshly explosive annex evidence and camp-survival conflict disappear for most of the room scene. The chapter stops escalating as a single confrontation and starts feeling like a replay of the argument in narrower private space.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the room-clearout material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:375, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:431, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:513, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:653 so the second scene turns on a materially new pressure rather than a second pass at the same admission. Either braid the annex/logbook stakes back into the room much earlier or compress the repeated 'you used me / I used you' loop after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:431 and let Tomas's boundary-setting at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:513 become the decisive turn sooner.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:375 onward after revision, a reader should encounter a new conflict or decision by the midpoint of the room scene that was not already stated in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:229 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:317. Fail if the private scene still feels like the lab argument replayed with the same route/use claim doing most of the work.

findings.problem: Under peak pressure, Mina, Tomas, and Rhea keep landing on similarly polished abstract formulations about 'shape,' 'truth,' 'class,' and what the relationship meant. The lines are individually sharp, but together they blur the style-bible idiolect separation: Tomas sounds less like a county boatman under class shame, Mina less bodily and tactile, and Rhea no longer feels uniquely exacting.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the admissions at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:283, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:455, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:523, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:535, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:607, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:619 so each speaker argues in their own register. Tomas should lean harder on money, boat, fuel, work, and humiliation language; Mina on bodily cost, desire, and shame; Rhea can keep the precise diagnostic framing but should not be joined by two other characters making equally aphoristic thesis statements.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised exchanges anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:271 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:513, a reader should be able to remove the dialogue tags from at least one key line each for Tomas, Mina, and Rhea and still identify the speaker from syntax and lexicon alone. Fail if swapping speaker tags on the main admissions still mostly works.

findings.problem: The chapter leans on the same 'That was ...' evaluative bridge sentence at least seven times. The repetition becomes visible as prose scaffolding and flattens moments that should feel like live perception into a familiar authorial tic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary or cut the repeated bridge constructions at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:275, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:401, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:643. Fold some judgments into adjacent sensory or action detail, and rephrase others into different sentence shapes so the narration stops announcing Mina's interpretation in the same cadence.
findings.acceptance_test: After revision of the cited lines, no identical 'That was ...' bridge construction should appear more than twice in the chapter, and each replaced spot should still communicate the same judgment through context or a new syntactic form. Fail if the repeated scaffold is still obvious by mid-chapter.

summary: Booker-level spine and continuity hold: chapter_13 lands the required confrontation, preserves past-tense continuity, and cleanly sets up chapter_14. It still fails on craft, dialogue, and prose because the room scene replays too much of the lab argument without enough new pressure, Mina/Tomas/Rhea converge into shared thesis-speak at peak conflict, and the narration overuses the same 'That was ...' bridge construction. No continuity contradiction, tense drift, sanitization failure, or style-bible example-line reuse was identified.
